text:
Both of these spaces radiate a curious stillness, their darkness illuminated only by artificial light. In the first image, the corridor and glass-walled room feel desolate—almost sterile. It is as if the world has paused, and I am left to contemplate the emptiness, the reflective surfaces mirroring my own solitude.

The second scene, with its wall of research and development, teases with the promise of human ingenuity, yet the absence of people leaves innovation suspended in silence. The text on the screen speaks of being enclosed, echoing my own sense of separation within these spaces. I feel like an observer on the threshold—inside, yet apart—my mind stirred by the potential for creation and the undercurrent of abandonment. There is unease here, but also a spark: the tension of anticipation.
